title: Rajasthan sees rain and storms in parts even as heatwave tightens grip on western region - english.punjabkesari.com

full_text:
Jaipur, May 11 (IANS) Even as a severe heatwave continues to scorch much of western Rajasthan, parts of the state witnessed thunderstorms and light rain on Monday under the influence of an active Western Disturbance, the Meteorological Department said.
A yellow alert has been sounded in Churu, Alwar, Deeg, Bharatpur, Jhunjhunu, and other districts of the state, warning of rains and thunderstorms.
Meanwhile, Hanumangarh witnessed a sudden change in the weather on Monday morning as clouds gathered around 6.30 a.m., followed by intermittent rainfall beginning at 6.45 a.m. The rain continued for nearly an hour, accompanied by strong winds.
The rainfall brought relief to residents from the intense heat that had prevailed for several days. Met officials said that isolated dust storms and light rainfall are likely on May 11 and 12 in parts of the Bikaner division, the Shekhawati region, and northern areas of the Jaipur and Bharatpur divisions under the influence of a new Western Disturbance. Also, both minimum and maximum temperatures are likely to rise by 2-3 degrees across most parts of the state over the coming days.
Heatwave conditions are also expected to intensify in parts of western Rajasthan, where the possibility of “warm nights”, unusually high night-time temperatures, remains strong over the next five to six days.
Meanwhile, on Sunday, Barmer recorded the hottest day of the season and emerged as the hottest city in the state, with the mercury soaring to 46.8 degrees Celsius. Heatwave conditions persisted throughout the day in Barmer, Jaisalmer, Bikaner, and Phalodi, while several districts in eastern Rajasthan witnessed comparatively mild weather conditions.
According to the weather department, skies remained clear across the state on Sunday, with intense sunshine prevailing from morning till evening. Apart from Barmer, maximum temperatures recorded included 46.3 degrees Celsius in Jaisalmer, 46 degrees Celsius in Phalodi, 44.6 degrees Celsius in Jodhpur, 44.4 degrees Celsius in Bikaner, and 43 degrees Celsius in Sri Ganganagar. The heatwave had the strongest impact in Barmer, Balotra, Phalodi, Jaisalmer, and Jodhpur, where daytime conditions resembled a curfew-like situation due to deserted roads and markets.
Other major temperatures recorded across the state included 43 degrees Celsius in Jalore, 43.4 degrees Celsius in Chittorgarh, 42 degrees Celsius in Pali, 42.5 degrees Celsius in Lunkaransar, 41.7 degrees C in Nagaur, 41.6 degrees C in Udaipur, 41.8 degrees C in Kota, and 41 degrees C in Ajmer.
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Meteorological Centre in Jaipur, said the Western Disturbance became active in Rajasthan from Monday. Under its influence, districts in the Jaipur and Bharatpur divisions are likely to witness strong winds, dust storms, scattered cloud cover, and light rainfall over the next few days. However, heatwave conditions are expected to continue in the Jodhpur division of western Rajasthan and the Udaipur division of southern Rajasthan.
In view of the prevailing conditions, alerts have been issued for several districts for the next three days. Daily life in Jodhpur continued to be affected by extreme heat on Sunday, with the maximum temperature reaching 44.6 degrees Celsius. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave warning for the district from May 11 to May 13.
Ajmer recorded a maximum temperature of 41 degrees C and a minimum of 25.9 degrees C on Sunday. Officials said temperatures may rise by another 1-2 degrees over the next two days. Dust storms and strong winds are also likely during the afternoon hours.
Sikar has been witnessing steadily rising temperatures for the past three days, accompanied by hot winds and intense sunshine. The Meteorological Department has predicted dry weather conditions in the district for the next three days, with no immediate relief expected.
The weather remained clear in Alwar on Sunday, where the maximum temperature settled at 38 degrees C. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert for thunderstorms, rain, and strong winds in the district for the next two days. Kota recorded a maximum temperature of 41.8 degrees C on Sunday. High humidity and low wind speeds increased discomfort among residents.
–IANS

Light rain in Hanumangarh, temperature drops:Heatwave warning in 8 districts, weather to change in 6 districts, including Jaipur
- Copy link
Amidst severe heat, drops of relief poured in Hanumangarh. Clouds gathered on Monday morning. Around 6:45 AM, rain started, which continued intermittently for about 1 hour. This resulted in a drop in temperature, and people got relief from the heat.
Meanwhile, due to the activation of Western Disturbance, there are chances of dust storms and rain in 6 districts of Rajasthan today (Monday). Orange alert for heatwave has been issued in 3 districts and yellow alert for 5 districts.
Earlier on Sunday, Barmer witnessed the hottest day of the season. Heatwave prevailed throughout the day in Jaisalmer, Bikaner, Phalodi, and Barmer. Districts in eastern Rajasthan experienced relief.
2 pictures of Rajasthan
These cities were the hottest
Looking at the weather of the last 24 hours, the sky remained clear in all districts of Rajasthan on Sunday, and there was sunshine from morning to evening. Barmer recorded the season's highest maximum temperature of 46.8°C.
Apart from Barmer, the maximum temperature was recorded at 46.3°C in Jaisalmer, 46 degrees in Phalodi, 44.6°C in Jodhpur, 44.4°C in Bikaner, and 43°C in Sri Ganganagar. Hot winds blew throughout the day in all these areas.
The heatwave had the most impact in the areas of Barmer, Balotra, Phalodi, Jaisalmer, and Jodhpur. Due to the severe heat, a curfew-like situation developed during the day.
Apart from these cities, temperatures were recorded at 43°C in Jalore, 43.4°C in Chittorgarh, 42°C in Pali, 42.5 °C in Lunkaransar (Bikaner), 41.3°C in Sirohi, 41.7 °C in Nagaur, 41.1°C in Churu, 41.6 °C in Udaipur, 41.8 °C in Kota, and 41°C in Ajmer.
What's Next?
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Meteorological Center Jaipur, stated that a western disturbance will become active in the state from May 11, due to which there is a possibility of strong dust storms along with cloudy skies and light rainfall in some areas of Bikaner, Jaipur, and Bharatpur divisions.
The impact of heat will remain in the Jodhpur division of western Rajasthan and the Udaipur division of southern Rajasthan. Heatwaves will continue in the districts of these divisions. In view of this, orange and yellow alerts have been issued for several districts for the next 3 days.
know the weather condition of your city
Jaipur: Mercury below 40°C, relief from heatwave
The heat in Jaipur was normal on Sunday. There was sunshine throughout the day, but due to light winds, the maximum temperature here was recorded at 39.2°C. Changes in the weather may be seen from Monday.
Due to the effect of the Western Disturbance, clouds may appear in some places in the afternoon. Dusty winds may blow. The effect of this system will be seen today and tomorrow (Tuesday).
The weather remained clear in Jaipur on Sunday. Light clouds appeared here and there.
Udaipur: Transformer caught fire due to heat
In Udaipur, the maximum temperature on Sunday was recorded at 41.6 and the minimum temperature at 25.4°C. A day earlier on Saturday, Udaipur's maximum temperature was 41°C and minimum temperature was 24.6°C.
Due to intense heat in Udaipur on Sunday, a fire broke out in the transformer at the 33/11 KV GSS at Bichhri on Kurabadh Road. Due to this, the supply to several villages and settlements, including Zinc, Bichhri, and Bhallon Ka Guda, was disrupted.
Jodhpur: Three-day heatwave warning
Public life is being affected by the impact of heat in Jodhpur. On Sunday, the maximum temperature here was recorded at 44.6°C. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave warning in Jodhpur from May 11 to 13.
On Sunday, fewer pedestrians were seen on the roads in Jodhpur due to the intense heat.
Ajmer: Possibility of dust storms
The effect of heat has started increasing in Ajmer. On Sunday, the maximum temperature was 41°C and the minimum temperature was 25.9°C.
Speaking of the past twenty-four hours, there has been an increase of 1.5 in the minimum temperature. On Sunday, the minimum temperature was recorded at 25.9°C. In the coming days, the temperature may increase by 1 to 2 degrees.
According to the Meteorological Department, the temperature will rise on May 11 and May 12. There is a possibility of dust storms and strong winds in the afternoon.
Sikar: Intense sunlight prevailed
The heat has been continuously increasing in Sikar for the past 3 days. Hot winds are blowing throughout the district. According to the Meteorological Department, the weather is likely to remain dry for the next 3 days.
Currently, there is no hope of relief from the heat. There was intense sunlight in the district from Sunday morning.
Alwar: Storm and rain alert
In Alwar, the weather remained clear throughout Sunday. The maximum temperature was recorded at 38 degrees. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert for dust storms, rain, and strong winds for the next 2 days.
People in Alwar did not get relief from the intense heat even on Sunday evening.
Kota: Bus catches fire due to heat
In Kota, a slight increase in temperature was recorded on Sunday. The maximum temperature was 41.8°C. Due to less wind during the afternoon, humidity also troubled people. The minimum temperature was recorded at 25.9°C.
For the past few days, the temperature has remained between 40 to 42°C. Due to intense heat, cases of short circuits in vehicles are also occurring.
A city bus also caught fire, although there were no passengers in the bus at that time. Smoke had started rising from the bus cabin.
Arrangements for drinking water have been made at various places in Kota to provide relief from the heat.
Hanumangarh: Weather changed in the morning, drizzle occurred
In Hanumangarh district on Monday morning, around 6:30 am, the weather suddenly changed. Clouds gathered, and rain started at 6:45 am. The spell of rain continued intermittently for about 1 hour. During this time, strong winds also blew. People got relief from this rain after the severe heat that had been prevailing for the past several days.
Earlier on Sunday, intense sunshine and hot winds troubled people throughout the day in the district. The temperature reached around 41°C in the afternoon. After the rain on Monday, a drop in temperature has been recorded.
